title: A postcard I once received
summary: Tell a small story about a postcard that someone once sent you.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a postcard you received. Say who sent it, what the picture was, what the message said, and how you felt. Use past simple.
hintStarters: Three years ago, a friend sent me…
The picture on the front was…
sampleText: Three years ago, a friend sent me a postcard from Krakow. I do not get many letters, so I was very surprised. I opened the small post box and I saw the white edge of the card. I took it out and I looked at the front. The picture was a quiet street in winter with low yellow lights. The snow on the roofs was very white. On the back, my friend wrote a short message in blue ink. Her handwriting was small and clean. She said the city was cold but very beautiful. She said she missed our long talks at the café. She wrote my name with a little heart next to it. I read the message two times. Then I sat down at the kitchen table and I smiled. I did not write back the same day. I waited one week and then I sent her a postcard from my own city. I stuck her card on the fridge with a small magnet. I see it every morning when I take out the milk.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gửi cho tôi một tấm bưu thiếp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the white edge of the card

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một con phố yên tĩnh vào mùa đông

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Her handwriting was small and clean
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chữ viết tay của cô ấy nhỏ và sạch
nhớ những cuộc nói chuyện dài

sampleAnnotations.phrase: stuck her card on the fridge
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: dán tấm thiếp lên tủ lạnh
bằng một viên nam châm nhỏ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a memory
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể về một kỷ niệm cụ thể, dùng quá khứ đơn: 'sent', 'opened', 'took', 'wrote'. Động từ bất quy tắc: send → sent, take → took, write → wrote, see → saw, sit → sat.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When telling a specific memory, use past simple: 'sent', 'opened', 'took', 'wrote'. Irregular verbs: send → sent, take → took, write → wrote, see → saw, sit → sat.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time expressions for past events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Three years ago', 'one week later', 'the same day' giúp người đọc biết khi nào câu chuyện xảy ra. Đặt ở đầu câu hoặc cuối câu.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Three years ago', 'one week later', 'the same day' tell the reader when the story happened. Place them at the start or end of the sentence.

sampleTranslationVi: Ba năm trước, một người bạn gửi cho tôi tấm bưu thiếp từ Krakow. Tôi không nhận được nhiều thư, nên tôi rất bất ngờ. Tôi mở hộp thư nhỏ và thấy mép trắng của tấm thiếp. Tôi lấy ra và nhìn mặt trước. Bức tranh là một con phố yên tĩnh vào mùa đông với những ánh đèn vàng thấp. Tuyết trên mái nhà rất trắng. Mặt sau, bạn tôi viết một lời nhắn ngắn bằng mực xanh. Chữ viết tay của cô ấy nhỏ và sạch. Cô ấy nói thành phố lạnh nhưng rất đẹp. Cô ấy nói cô nhớ những cuộc nói chuyện dài ở quán cà phê. Cô viết tên tôi với một trái tim nhỏ bên cạnh. Tôi đọc lời nhắn hai lần. Rồi tôi ngồi xuống bàn bếp và mỉm cười. Tôi không viết trả lời ngay ngày đó. Tôi đợi một tuần và rồi gửi cho cô ấy một tấm bưu thiếp từ thành phố mình. Tôi dán tấm thiếp lên tủ lạnh bằng một viên nam châm nhỏ. Tôi nhìn thấy nó mỗi sáng khi lấy sữa.

mcQuestions.prompt: When did the writer receive the postcard?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Three years ago, a friend sent me a postcard'.
The text says 'a friend sent me a postcard from Krakow'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What was the picture on the front?
options: A quiet street in winter with low yellow lights
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The picture was a quiet street in winter with low yellow lights'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the description of the front.

mcQuestions.prompt: What colour ink did the friend use?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'my friend wrote a short message in blue ink'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the friend say in the message?
options: The city was cold but very beautiful, and she missed their long talks.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says she said the city 'was cold but very beautiful' and she 'missed our long talks at the café'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the writer put the postcard?
options: On the fridge with a small magnet
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I stuck her card on the fridge with a small magnet'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the kitchen sentence near the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'write'.
Which sentence uses past simple correctly?
options: I take it out and looked at the front.
I took it out and I looked at the front.
I taking it out and I look at the front.
I takes it out and I looking at the front.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs should be in past simple: 'took' and 'looked'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the tense in both clauses.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did the writer wait before sending a reply?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I waited one week and then I sent her a postcard'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is a correct past time expression?
options: I sent her a postcard one week later.
I sent her a postcard one week to.
I sent her a postcard in one week ago.
I sent her a postcard one week future.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'one week later' is a common past time phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick a natural English time phrase about a past event.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does a paper postcard often feel more special than a quick digital message?
